source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the drivetrain frame of a student-robotics competition robot for a 2025 edition, delivered as a multi-part assembly because a drivetrain frame of this class is by construction a built-up aluminum-extrusion + laser-cut-sheet structure composed of a base rail frame, side uprights, a top cross-brace, wheel/motor mount plates, and handhold/gantry-lift interfaces. Self-weight vertical shock, tipping-recovery, and 4-point lifting pass/fail all reference the assembled frame geometry.

The robot must fit within a stowed-configuration envelope of approximately 1000 mm x 1000 mm x 1500 mm (L x W x H). The combined mass of all robots per team must not exceed 50 kg (if one robot, all mass budget applies; if manual+automatic robots, the combined mass must remain under 50 kg). The primary battery is limited to 24 V nominal. Any pneumatic actuators must operate at a gauge pressure no greater than 600 kPa (0.6 MPa) and must be supplied from a reservoir certified for that pressure.

Assembly layout. (A) Base rail frame: rectangular extrusion base carrying drivetrain and acting as primary load path for self-weight and lifting. (B) Side upright: vertical extrusion columns rising from base to the top frame (>= 4 for a rectangular frame). (C) Top cross-brace: transverse extrusion tying the uprights at the top; reacts the horizontal tipping-recovery force. (D) Wheel/motor mount plate: laser-cut sheet bolted to the base rail, carrying wheel axles and drive motors. (E) Handhold / gantry-lift interface: 4 discrete handhold brackets (or gantry rail tabs) bonded/bolted at the top frame or uprights for tournament lifting.

The drivetrain frame must carry (a) the robot's own self-weight (40 kg assumed for a single-robot design) under a 2 g vertical shock equivalent (80 kg equivalent) with no permanent yield, (b) a 200 N horizontal tipping-recovery force applied at the top of the robot, (c) four-point lifting via hand-holds or a gantry interface where each hand-hold carries 20 kg under 1.5 g shock, representing tournament handling.

Assembly policy. Mass cap (R1) and envelope (R2) are whole-frame geometric/mass checks. Self-weight 2 g yield (R3 / LC1) depends on base + upright + motor-mount coupling. Tipping-recovery (LC2) is the top cross-brace reacting into uprights into base - an assembled-frame load path. 4-point lifting (LC3) enters at the handhold brackets and redistributes through uprights into the base. No part evaluated alone has meaningful pass/fail.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: linear static structural FEA for the frame under the three load cases. Pneumatic reservoir pressure certification and voltage rating are regulatory / component-specification checks (non-FEA). Vibration, thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are explicitly NOT required at the mechanical design gate.

Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the drivetrain frame, a mass budget, and a report listing total robot mass in kg, max von Mises stress per load case in MPa, and max frame deflection per load case in mm.
short_prompt:
Design the drivetrain frame of a student-robotics competition robot for a 2025 edition, delivered as a built-up multi-part assembly of aluminum extrusions and laser-cut sheet: base rail frame + >= 4 side uprights + top cross-brace + wheel/motor mount plates + 4 handhold/gantry-lift brackets. Stowed envelope ~1000 x 1000 x 1500 mm (LxWxH). Per-team combined robot mass <= 50 kg. Primary battery <= 24 V nominal. Pneumatics <= 600 kPa (0.6 MPa) from a reservoir certified at that pressure.

Load cases.
- LC1 self-weight vertical shock: 40 kg frame under 2 g vertical (equivalent 80 kg) with no permanent yield.
- LC2 tipping-recovery: 200 N horizontal at the top of the robot.
- LC3 4-point lifting: each of 4 handholds carrying 20 kg under 1.5 g shock.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: 24 V battery and 600 kPa pneumatic cert are catalog / datasheet lookups, not simulation.

prompt.title: Student robotics drivetrain frame as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: Drivetrain frame fitting 1 m x 1 m x 1.5 m: base rail + side uprights + top cross-brace + wheel/motor mount plates + handhold/gantry tabs; total robot mass <= 50 kg, 24 V battery, pneumatic <= 600 kPa.

parts.name: base_rail_frame
parts.role: rectangular extrusion base forming primary lower load path

parts.name: side_upright
parts.role: vertical extrusion columns tying base to top frame

parts.name: top_cross_brace
parts.role: transverse extrusion at top reacting tipping-recovery force

parts.name: wheel_motor_mount_plate
parts.role: laser-cut sheet carrying wheel axles and drive motors

parts.name: handhold_bracket
parts.role: handhold or gantry-lift interface for tournament handling

load_cases.name: lifting_handhold
load_cases.description: 20 kg x 1.5 g per handhold, 4 points

boundary_conditions.wheels: fixed at contact for LC1, LC2

output_format.body_count: multi-body (base rail + uprights + top brace + mount plates + handholds)

interfaces.load_path: self-weight and lifting reactions between base and uprights
tipping-recovery horizontal force reacted from top into uprights into base
wheel contact and drive reactions into base rail
4-point lifting inertia enters frame at handholds and redistributes into uprights and base

fem_check_policy.rationale: Envelope and mass cap are whole-frame geometric/mass checks. Self-weight vertical shock, tipping-recovery, and 4-point lifting yield checks all involve load paths spanning base rail, uprights, top cross-brace, and handhold brackets - assembly-scope quantities.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: A student-robotics drivetrain frame is intrinsically an extrusion + sheet-metal assembly; rulebook mass cap, envelope, tipping, and lifting all reference assembled geometry.

eval_coverage: envelope + mass cap on assembled frame
tipping recovery static via top cross-brace to base path
handling / lifting static at 4 handhold interfaces
